What can I include in my heart healthy plan? Ask your dietitian or healthcare provider how many servings to have
from each of the following food groups:
1. Grains: 
○ Whole-wheat breads, cereals, and pastas, and brown rice
○ Low-fat, low-sodium crackers and chips
2. Vegetables: 
○ Broccoli, green beans, green peas, and spinach
○ Collards, kale, and lima beans
○ Carrots, sweet potatoes, tomatoes, and peppers
○ Canned vegetables with no salt added
3. Fruits: 
○ Bananas, peaches, pears, and pineapple
○ Grapes, raisins, and dates
○ Oranges, tangerines, grapefruit, orange juice, and grapefruit juice
○ Apricots, mangoes, melons, and papaya
○ Raspberries and strawberries
○ Canned fruit with no added sugar

[DOB-4819] - Office Visit in Norton Community Medical Associates - Barret (continued)
Patient Instructions (continued)
4. Low-fat dairy: 
○ Nonfat (skim) milk, 1% milk, and low-fat almond, cashew, or soy milks fortified with calcium
○ Low-fat cheese, regular or frozen yogurt, and cottage cheese
5. Meats and proteins: 
○ Lean cuts of beef and pork (loin, leg, round), skinless chicken and turkey
○ Legumes, soy products, egg whites, or nuts
What should I limit or not include in my heart healthy plan? 
1. Unhealthy fats and oils: 
○ Whole or 2% milk, cream cheese, sour cream, or cheese
○ High-fat cuts of beef (T-bone steaks, ribs), chicken or turkey with skin, and organ meats such as liver
○ Butter, stick margarine, shortening, and cooking oils such as coconut or palm oil
2. Foods and liquids high in sodium: 
○ Packaged foods, such as frozen dinners, cookies, macaroni and cheese, and cereals with more than
300 mg of sodium per serving
○ Vegetables with added sodium, such as instant potatoes, vegetables with added sauces, or regular
canned vegetables
○ Cured or smoked meats, such as hot dogs, bacon, and sausage
○ High-sodium ketchup, barbecue sauce, salad dressing, pickles, olives, soy sauce, or miso
3. Foods and liquids high in sugar: 
○ Candy, cake, cookies, pies, or doughnuts
○ Soft drinks (soda), sports drinks, or sweetened tea
○ Canned or dry mixes for cakes, soups, sauces, or gravies
What other guidelines should I follow for a healthy heart? ·Do not smoke. Nicotine and other chemicals in cigarettes and cigars can cause lung and heart damage. Ask
your healthcare provider for information if you currently smoke and need help to quit. E-cigarettes or smokeless
tobacco still contain nicotine. Talk to your healthcare provider before you use these products. ·Limit or do not drink alcohol as directed. Alcohol can damage your heart and raise your blood pressure. Your healthcare provider may give you specific daily and weekly limits. The general recommended limit is 1

drink a day for women 21 or older and for men 65 or older. Do not have more than 3 drinks in a day or 7 in a
week. The recommended limit is 2 drinks a day for men 21 to 64 years of age. Do not have more than 4 drinks
in a day or 14 in a week. A drink of alcohol is 12 ounces of beer, 5 ounces of wine, or 1½ ounces of liquor. ·Exercise regularly. Exercise can help you maintain a healthy weight and improve your blood pressure and
cholesterol levels. Regular exercise can also decrease your risk for heart problems. Ask your healthcare
provider about the best exercise plan for you. Do not start an exercise program without asking your 
healthcare provider.

Called pt,

papers signed and stamp by the provider. Pt states that she was told that the original paper work that was given to the
employer were not clear on why the pt needed the accommodation she states. Pt states that her employer needs more details as to why she needs that length of time. Pt states that she has a few
more forms that she was given from her employer that PCP needs to fill out as well. Pt states that she will drop them
off tomorrow. Explained to pt that PCP was not in the office today. Once PCP is back in the office she will review this message and
someone will give her call back.

Clinical Notes
Injured the left shoulder while at work
Here with significant other (Robert Knapper) who is also my patient
She works at Amazon and she notes that there was a injury following a tub falling on her when she went to move
some items. She notes that it fell on her shoulders on exam her right shoulder is tender on the anterior aspect and on
the left side her left shoulder is tender on the anterior and posterior aspect and she has a red erythematous rash on
the left shoulder. Given patient's medications recommended we do a course of Medrol Dosepak to help with the inflammation advised
patient to take a muscle relaxant at nighttime. And also recommended triamcinolone cream to help with the rash
Adjusted the physical therapy order
Lives with significant other
Has 2 kids- daughter lives in GA, son lives in OR
Originally from Pennsylvania, then moved to Oregon and now in KY
Is also a CNA

Message from Dr. Monalisa Tailor: Thanks for getting your testing done. Your kidney and liver function look good. Your electrolytes are normal. Your vitamin B12 is normal. Your A1c (3 month marker for diabetes) is still in the prediabetic range with an average 3 month blood sugar of 130. This is considered prediabetes between 5.7-6.4%. >>I would recommend limiting carbohydrates 25 grams/per meal for women, 35 grams/per meal for men to help with
your glucose. >>I would recommend cutting down on sweets, sodas, alcohol to help with limiting carbohydrates in your nutrition. >>There may also be resources available from your employer to help with prediabetes. There's also a video series from Norton Healthcare to help with pre-diabetes as well with the information below.

Message from Dr. Monalisa Tailor: Thanks for getting your testing done. Your hemoglobin and blood counts look good, and you are not anemic. Your kidney and liver function look good. Your electrolytes are normal. Your thyroid is normal. Your A1c (3 month marker for diabetes) is still in the prediabetic range with an average 3 month blood sugar of 135. This is considered prediabetes between 5.7-6.4%. >>I would recommend limiting carbohydrates 25 grams/per meal for women, 35 grams/per meal for men to help with
your glucose. >>I would recommend cutting down on sweets, sodas, alcohol to help with limiting carbohydrates in your nutrition. >>There may also be resources available from your employer to help with prediabetes. There's also a video series from Norton Healthcare to help with pre-diabetes as well with the information below. Thank you for trusting Norton Healthcare with your health and wellness! You are receiving this message because
you have been referred to Norton's Prediabetes On-demand Video Series. Below you will find the link or QR Code to register for our class to begin learning how to improve your health and
prevent Type 2 Diabetes. If you have any questions after watching the series, please send an email to
[EMAIL-6334] to connect with one of our Certified Diabetes Care & Education Specialists
(CDCES). We also welcome any feedback you are willing to share about our program. Scan or visit the website below to get started!

- a phone call back. - a status update. All contact information has been validated and/or updated in the contact section of this encounter.

by Dr Samji's office today and get update on the alternative treatment for her Osteoporosis.

is requesting to have forms updated to state:
